title: Write launch faq for sales and success
description: Real-world PM scenario on write launch FAQ for sales and success with conflicting stakeholder incentives and incomplete data.
scenarioBrief: You are a PM at AutoLane, a Series A workflow automation company (98 employees, $7.0M ARR). Your immediate assignment is write launch FAQ for sales and success. The executive team expects a recommendation before Friday's review. You have four context sources: a KPI snapshot, stakeholder notes, customer voice, and execution risk data. Each source is useful but incomplete, and at least one source is biased or contradictory. Optimize for repeatable outcomes over one-off wins that do not scale.

contextMaterials.title: KPI Snapshot (Last 90 Days)
contextMaterials.content:
Monthly active accounts: 40822
New logo adds: 2119
Activation rate: 31%
30-day retention: 36%
NPS: 1 (n=161)
Expansion rate in top cohort: 6%
Quarter-over-quarter activation delta: 6 points
Quarter-over-quarter retention delta: -6 points
Caveat: NPS sample over-indexes power users and excludes the most recent downgraded accounts.

contextMaterials.title: Stakeholder Notes
contextMaterials.content:
CRO (Zane Morris): "I need a plan sales can explain in live calls next week, not a roadmap promise."
VP Engineering (Anya Carter): "We can move quickly if every blocker has an owner and fallback path."
Head of Customer Success (Noah Nguyen): "High-noise accounts dominate feedback threads, but silent churn in mid-market is the bigger risk."
Commercial note: 9 late-stage deals cite this area as a decision factor.
Support note: 43 escalations in the last month mention related pain points.
Bias warning: projected revenue impact is directional and not contract-backed.

contextMaterials.title: Customer Voice (Interview Snippets)
contextMaterials.content:
Pinecrest Retail (Operations Lead): "We see value quickly once configured, but the first session still feels too heavy."
Atlas Commerce (IT Manager): "Metric definitions changed between reports, which reduced confidence in decision meetings."
Orbit Travel Co (Director of Product): "The concept is right, but edge-case handling still feels unfinished."
Data gap: support transcript tagging is inconsistent across segments.

contextMaterials.title: Delivery and Risk Snapshot
contextMaterials.content:
Open P1/P2 issues: 33
Median cycle time: 10 days
On-time delivery last quarter: 61%
Shared platform dependencies unresolved: 10
Release rollback events last quarter: 4
Average time to resolve escalations: 70 hours
Contradiction: roadmap shows full utilization, but squads report 10-20% interrupt-driven bandwidth.

submissionFields.hint: Map each audience to the message they need and why.
submissionFields.label: Audience and Intent

submissionFields.hint: Write the core narrative that aligns stakeholders on action.
submissionFields.label: Core Narrative

submissionFields.hint: Predict objections from key stakeholders and how you will respond.
submissionFields.label: Expected Pushback

submissionFields.hint: Draft the memo or all-hands update you would send.
submissionFields.label: Message Draft

submissionFields.hint: Explain how you will collect feedback and close open issues.
submissionFields.label: Follow-up and Feedback Loop

rubric.name: Audience Fit
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is messaging adapted to the needs and incentives of each audience?

rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is communication clear, direct, and decision-oriented?

rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Does it handle disagreement constructively and concretely?

rubric.name: Credibility
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are claims grounded in evidence with transparent caveats?

expertSolution.content: Executives need decision logic and downside exposure, delivery teams need concrete scope and ownership, and customer-facing teams need timing and expectation framing. Tailor message depth by audience without changing the core narrative.

Recommendation: align on a staged plan because the data is mixed, not broken. Activation is 31% and retention is 36%, while NPS (1, n=161) and 43 escalations indicate trust risk if execution quality slips.

Expect commercial pressure from Zane Morris around the 9 deal pipeline, technical caution from Anya Carter on dependency risk (10 unresolved), and customer-impact pressure from Noah Nguyen due to escalation volume. Address each with explicit thresholds and review dates.

Team, we are moving to a bounded plan that prioritizes reliability and measurable customer value. Baseline today is 40822 active accounts, activation 31%, retention 36%, and on-time delivery 61%. We will publish weekly progress and revisit scope at the midpoint checkpoint.

Hold a structured Q&A within 24 hours, capture unresolved questions in a decision log, and assign owner plus due date for each item. Publish weekly updates that close the loop on objections raised by Sales, Engineering, and Customer Success.
